1: And And bow thine ear to my understanding:
2: And that that thy lips may keep knowledge.
3: And her mouth is is smoother than oil:
5: Her steps take hold on hell.
6: Her ways are moveable, that that thou canst not know them them .
7: And depart not from the words of my mouth.
8: And come not nigh the door of her house:
9: And thy years unto the cruel:
10: And thy labours be be in the house of a stranger;
11: When thy flesh and thy body are consumed,
13: Nor inclined mine ear to them that instructed me!
14: In the midst of the congregation and assembly.
15: And running waters out of thine own well.
16: And And rivers of waters in the streets.
18: And rejoice with the wife of thy youth.
19: And be thou ravished always with her love.
20: And embrace the bosom of a stranger?
21: And he pondereth all his goings.
22: And he shall be holden with the cords of his sins.
23: And in the greatness of his folly he shall go astray.
